entry.title: Re: Help Needed Enabling Data Builder in SAP Data Sphere
entry.content: Thank you mark You are for the quick support .

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

This is a great initiative and it would be great if I can be part of the Digital Skills Initiative program.

 
I identify myself to be a good fit for this initiative on the below aspects:
  • I am a woman
  • First generation college graduate in my family. First woman to graduate in Engineering and Business administration in my family.
  • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts
  • People looking to switch their careers mid-life

I am basically from India where I was working as a SAP MM Consultant. Recently I moved to Germany into a new role as SAP MDG consultant during my mid-life career and might return back to India.  Currently I'm expecting a child, which may leave a career gap due to maternity leave. I would like to use this Digital Skills initiative effectively to manage the career gap wisely.

This opportunity would be very helpful for me in learning more about SAP and getting a certification.

 
Hoping to hear from you soon. I have also turned on the private messaging option.
 
Thanks and Regards,
Marie Therese Jenifer L

entry.title: Re: Can we use AMDP in our projects?
entry.content: Interesting question. I would like to know more about this as well. In my company we barelly consider AMDP as an option...

entry.title: Re: SAP BPA Error Happening in Monitoring - Requesting help!
entry.content: @Ron_Hagan I want to pass Environment variables in a Process flow in Build Process automation how can I do that ?

entry.title: Re: CI/CD Process Failed With Error
entry.content:
Hi,

The pipeline tries to run the npm run build command and fails there. The application does not seem to be set up correctly to run this. For example: Do you have a ui5.yaml? Maybe this can help: https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/v3/pages/GettingStarted/

We are not ui5 experts either, so it would probably be best to reach out to the UI5 community.

If you just want the pipeline to run then you may rename the "build" script in the package.json to something else, e.g. "ui5-build", and it will not run the build, but only do an npm install and continue.

Hope that helps,
Oliver

entry.title: Re: Decisions Made Simple in SAP Build Process Automation
entry.content:
@Archana How can I pass Environment variables to a Process flow in Build Process automation ? 

Could you please help with that

entry.title: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflow through Business Application Studio?
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Greetings!

I have a requirement to trigger Build Process Automation from Custom UI5 application developed in BAS. I have gone through multiple tutorials but all are suggesting the triggering of BPA from Build Apps. 

Please guide me on this.

Thanks & Regards,

Ayushi Shrivastava

entry.title: Re: Vendor Sub-Ranges Functionality
entry.content:
Hi

very informative..In summary one can maintain multiple payment terms,incoterms,currencies for same BP through this functionality. This also needs to be supported with multiple addresses right ? Do we have that for supplier  in S/4 ? Same supplier supplying from multiple countries with different payment terms,delivery dates,order currencies should also have the ability to store multiple addresses so that right vendor subrange gets triggered with right address. Maintaining multiple BP for same supplier with multiple location will defy the purpose of vendor subrange functionality.

entry.title: Re: extension of table fields visible length-its urgent
entry.content:
Hello ,

Thank you for the clear explanation and it is working fine.

Best Regards,

Roja.

entry.title: Enhancement for invoice to invoice correction request (invoice to order).
entry.content:
Hi All,

I have a requirement as below:

When an invoice correction request is created with reference to an invoice, particular condition type should be re-determined. Since the standard pricing type for invoice to invoice correction request is "E - Copy price elements and fix values" for credit item (invoice correction req - sales doc type is K), even if a condition record is deleted, it is still being copied from reference invoice to invoice correction.

Where can we put an enhancement (any exits or copy control routines) for re-determining a condition type from invoice to invoice correction request?

entry.title: Re: Be Comfortable, Authentic and Interview the Interviewer 🧑🏻‍💻
entry.content: Excellent insight Thanks for sharing

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Plain "REST" endpoint (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content:
Hello,
Good explanation of the difference between Odata V4 and REST!

My submission:

entry.title: Re: Ariba : User exit for ARBCIG_INVOICE
entry.content:
Hello, 

Please check if this helps: 

### User Exits
1. **EXIT_SAPLVEDF_001 (Customer Functions in IDoc Interface for Store Data)**
- Use this User Exit to modify IDoc data before the IDoc is stored in the database.
2. **EXIT_SAPLV56K_002 (Customer Enhancements for Outbound Processing)**
- Use this for enhancements during outbound processing of IDocs, which might be a place to modify or remove segments.
3. **EXIT_SAPLV02Z_004 (User Exit for IDoc from database during update task)**
- This user exit allows you to modify IDocs fetched from the database during the update task.

 

### BADIs
1. **BADI_IDOC_OUTPUT_PROCESSING (Output Processing for IDocs)**
- With this BADI, you can implement adjustments to the IDoc during the output processing phase.
2. **BADI_EDOC_COCKPIT_IDOC**
- This BADI allows custom business logic during IDoc processing.

### Enhancement Spots
1. **IDOC_OUTPUT**
- This enhancement spot is specifically designed for adjusting IDoc data during output processing.

 

BR
Harish Mangtani

entry.title: Re: CAP Table and Column Names are all in Uppercase after Build
entry.content: Hi Hans,

entry.title: Re: Navigating Regulatory Changes with Joule
entry.content: Great use of GenAI!

entry.title: Re: CAP Table and Column Names are all in Uppercase after Build
entry.content: Hi Hans- Joachim, I was trying to create a Function import which returns the array of the entity types. Here we had the same issue of upper case in the HANA DB. We had to manually map the values to Camel case and send it to response as direct mapping didn't work. Is there any way to simplify this so that we dont have to map each and every field of the entity to camel case. Thanks & Regards, Srinivasan

entry.title: Re: Task 2 - Capire's Hello World! (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content: My submission for task 1&2

entry.title: How DataSphere push and pull data to SAP PaPM Cloud - Universal Model via Replication Flow
entry.content:
Hey everyone, I am back again and this time with SAP Profitability and Performance Management Cloud (SAP PaPM Cloud) - Universal Model (UM) integration 😀

You may occasionally receive integration questions like:
a. I have my input data available in DataSphere, how can I push it to UM?
b. Now I get the results of my UM calculation logic, how can I pull this back to Datasphere?

I wanted to share with you one solution to answer these questions hence providing you with a how to guide on how DataSphere leverages the Replication Flow in order to perform outbound and inbound of  data to and from SAP PaPM Cloud – UM.

Prerequisite:

1. In your DataSphere Tenant Connections section, Create SAP HANA Connection.

a. Choose Create Connection
b. Choose Feature > Replication Flows
c. Choose SAP HANA

2. In the popup which appears, fill in the information needed then on the next step feed business name which will be the description of the connection.

Note: Database information and credentials are available from SAP PaPM Cloud Standard Model > Administration > Settings. Use SAP_PAPM_ADMIN as it inherently has database privileges to update data of Z generated tables from SAP_PAPM_UM.

a. For Host, use Database Host.
b. For Port, use Database Port
c. For User Name, use Database user name
d. For Password, use Database user password

3. Choose Next Steps and finish creating connection. Connection will now be available for usage.

How to push data to Universal Model?

Note: Idea is that the data will be pushed directly to Z_<ENV>_<FUNCTIONID> table of Model Entity generated in Universal Model, hence make the fields including primary key and data types consistent from source table in datasphere. Else you can replicate the source table to your preferred schema and access it via SAP PaPM Cloud - UM HANA Schema Connections.

Now let’s start 😊

  1. Local object (e.g. table) is available in DataSphere which will act as source object.

2. Once data is prepared, Create replication flow in DataSphere.

a. Choose Data Builder
b. Choose Create Replication Flow
c. Choose Select Source Connection

d. Choose SAP DataSphere
e. Choose Add Source Objects

f. Choose the local table created in previous step.
g. Choose OK. In this step, source object can be seen available on the left hand side of Replication Flow Screen.

3. Select target object from SAP_PAPM_UM container

a. Choose Button for selecting target connection
b. Select the created connection to Universal Model
c. Choose Button for selecting target container

d. Choose SAP_PAPM_UM container
e. Choose the three dots
f. Select map to Existing Target Object
g. Search for the function name or Z artifact to which data from DataSphere be loaded into. Choose the UM generated artifact.
Note: Environment with Model Entity in UM should be activated for Z artifact to appear in the list.
h. Choose Next then on the next page choose Add Selection.

4. Perform mapping of fields

a. Choose the target object for mapping option to appear
b. On the properties panel on the right > Projection > Choose Add button
c. Add name for mapping
d. Choose Mapping Button
e. Perform mapping of source and target fields.
f. Choose Save
g. Deploy the replication flow
h. Run the replication flow

5. After competion of running replication flow, check database artifact or Show Data of Function where data is pushed into. Data is now available in Universal Model.

 

How to pull data from Universal Model?

1. After calculation logic, resulting data is available from Universal Model.

2. Given the knowledge we learned from push scenario, Create Replication Flow in Datasphere but this time UM will be the source and Datasphere will be the target. Deploy then Run.

Note: In this example, I replicated the UM table into Datasphere by taking over the whole table with a little adjustment on the naming preference hence no mapping into an existing datasphere table happened. If a local table is available as target table like in push scenario you can still proceed with doing so just like in push scenario.

3. Data from Universal Model is now available in Datasphere for further usage.

In conclusion, the integration scenario presented above illustrates one way of making  SAP PaPM Cloud Universal Model Data <> DataSphere available for each other’s usage. As a disclaimer i'm not a Datasphere expert and there can be more optimized way to do this but this example is worth sharing as far as my investigation is concerned 😊 I hope this example inspires you to explore other integration options. You’ll hear from me again soon!

entry.title: Generate Process Models with GenAI
entry.content:
Abstract

This blog post describes how to use Generative AI to create business processes from a graphic or a textual description as a BPMN file and import them into SAP Cloud ALM or SAP Signavio. Anyone can try the examples without any programming knowledge.

Content

  • Introduction
  • Process Authoring in SAP Cloud ALM
  • Use Case 1: Text based Process Description
  • Use Case 2: MS Excel Upload
  • Use Case 3: Create from a BPMN graphic or any other graphical representation of a process model
  • Use Case 4: Create from Sketch
  • Process Modeling with SAP Signavio
  • Comparison of AI models used
  • Conclusion

Introduction

Generative AI has taken the world by storm and is set to change the way we communicate and work forever. SAP Business AI is an excellent example of how this new technology can help businesses become more innovative and productive. The challenge now is to translate the amazing capabilities of generative AI into real-world solutions.

One use case is to generate business processes from textual descriptions or graphics. In this blog, I will show you some examples and how to use the generated process models in SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Signavio. I used ChatGPT 4 and Claude 3.5 for this purpose. The terms "Large Language Model (LLM)" and "Generative AI" are often used interchangeably, but they refer to different concepts within the field of artificial intelligence. From here on, I will use the term LLM when talking about generative AI to make the text more readable. See the section Comparison of AI models used for more information.

There is an important note to consider before you try out the examples with your own process models: With the standard generative AIs that are available to the public, such as ChatGPT or Claude 3.5, you cannot be sure that your data will not be used for training purposes. Internal business critical data or processes should not be used.

The SAP Generative AI Hub provides you with secure access for the use of your business-critical or internal data. I recommend the blog post How SAP's Generative AI Hub facilitates embedded, trustworthy, and reliable AI by @andreas_roth as an introduction.

Process Authoring in SAP Cloud ALM

SAP Cloud ALM is SAP's new cloud-based application lifecycle management platform that helps you to implement and operate intelligent cloud and hybrid business solutions. SAP Cloud ALM can be used without additional license costs. It is already included in SAP Cloud Service subscriptions and SAP Enterprise Support. Customers can also try SAP Cloud ALM for free in the public demo tenant.

In the Process Authoring app, you can create and maintain custom solution processes. In addition, there are three other options for the import of process models into SAP Cloud ALM:

  • API: Process models can be exchanged in SVG or BPMN format via a Rest API. The SAP Signavio Connector for SAP Cloud ALM (Consulting Solution, Community Edition) uses the API to transfer process models, for instance.
  • SVG file: The scalable vector graphics format is an xml-based format to describe vector-based images. If you import a process model as an SVG graphic into SAP Cloud ALM, it can only be displayed but no longer edited.
  • BPMN file: The Business Process Model and Notation (BPMN 2.0) is used to describe and visualize business processes in an interoperable, and interchangeable way. BPMN process models can be exchanged between programs via a standardized XML format.

Create a business process in SAP Cloud ALM from a BPMN file

I will use the BPMN import for my examples.

Text Based Process Description

Let's take a very simple process description for the first example:

  • Process name: Order Process
  • Actors: Customer, Sales, Stock
  • Activities:
    1. Customer: Place an order
    2. Sales: Confirm order
    3. Stock: Pack order, send order

For the first demo, I used ChatGPT-4 via the SAP AI Launchpad . SAP AI Launchpad is a multitenant software as a service (SaaS) application in SAP Business Technology Platform. A restricted free tier plan is available for trial and exploration purposes. But you can also use the openai web interface or an app - just make sure that ChatGPT-4 is used.

SAP AI Launchpad: A simple process generated by ChatGPT

The trick in the prompt is the second part about the bpmndi:BPMNDiagram section:

Generate BPMN xml code that I can import into a bpmn editor. Don't forget the bpmndi\:BPMNDiagram section to ensure that the graphical information is included.

Otherwise, the BPMN code is valid, but nothing is displayed when importing into SAP Cloud ALM. The result was copied into an editor, saved with the suffix .bpmn2 and uploaded to SAP Cloud ALM. This is the result:

Simple Order Process, generated by ChatGPT

The first experiment was successful, and I was able to show that you can generate correct BPMN2 xml code with a LLM and upload it to SAP Cloud ALM. You can also use a LLMs for brainstorming - just enter the process description without instructions and ChatGPT will suggest what a business process could look like. Now let's explore another use case with a slightly more complex process.

MS Excel upload

You would be surprised how often MS Excel is used to model or describe business processes as a spreadsheet. This is a typical example of a process description as a table:

Process "Order to Ship" as a table

 I saved the table as a .csv, loaded it this time into Claude 3.5 and entered this prompt:

This file contains a table that describes a business process.
Use this file to generate BPMN xml code that I can import into a bpmn editor.
Don't forget the bpmndi:BPMNDiagram section to ensure that the graphical information is included.

The generated bpmn code was saved with the suffix .bpmn2 and uploaded to SAP Cloud ALM. And here's the result:

Process "Order to Ship", generated by Claude 3.5

This result was not edited in SAP Cloud ALM but generated in the same way.

Create from a BPMN graphic or any other graphical representation of a process model

More recently, LLMs have become available that can analyze and interpret images. These are referred to as multimodal LLM or Vision Language Model (VLM). Examples include GPT-4o, Claude 3.5 sonnet and LLaVA (Large Language and Vision Assistant). These new models are both magical and a little scary. Upload a photo of a place or your last restaurant visit and ask where it is then you'll understand what I mean.

In my experience, the two most common "process modeling tools" are MS Excel and MS Project. Neither is suited for the job, but that's how it is. Here's one example of a process model in MS Project:

Process "Cab Booking Request" in MS PowerPoint

As you can see, this model is a bit more complex. With the usual prompt, only the lanes (customer, travel agent, taxi driver) were visible. For this reason, I have extended the prompt one more time.

Analyze the picture.
Generate BPMN xml code that I can import into a bpmn editor.
Don't forget the bpmndi\:BPMNDiagram section to ensure that the graphical information is included.
Provide a complete BPMN XML with the necessary BPMN DI elements for all shapes and connections.

 SAP Cloud ALM can easily import the generated XML code but does not recognize message flows. That's why I used SAP Signavio this time:

Process "Cab Booking Request", generated with Claude 3.5, imported in SAP Signavio

Not perfect, but you can fix the visual layout within seconds. There is another interesting detail in the example. The original graphic contains an error: A Message Flow cannot be linked to a Start Event; the correct way would be to use a Start Message Event. Claude 3.5 has created a correct xml in this case by creating the message flow for the subsequent task. While I was testing, it often happened that LLM automatically corrected wrong models.

Another idea is to create BPMN xml from other visual representation. A further option would be to generate BPMN XML code from other graphical process descriptions, such as flowcharts or eEPKs. Let me know in the comments if this works for you.

Create from Sketch

Let's move on to the last, and I think most exciting, example. When a team creates a new business process or adapts an existing one, they typically don't model with a BMPN editor, but with a whiteboard or flipchart. Getting creative is more important than getting the modeling right. Nevertheless, someone must then transfer the sketch into a BPMN model. This tedious manual work can be made a bit easier.

The idea of automatically transforming hand-drawn business process models into BPMN code is not a new one. In 2021, the first ALMathon took place as part of the ALM Summit EMEA. The winner was the BPMN creator from Team Vostura, who was able to do this using traditional programming methods. ChatGPT was still relatively unknown at the time. The big hype did not start until the end of 2022, with the release of version 3.5, which was free for everyone to use.

Let's look at how an LLM performs this task. This image was drawn on a whiteboard, photographed, and exported as a JPEG.

Process "Contract Request" as a sketch on a whiteboard

 The image is uploaded to Claude 3.5. After that, the familiar prompt is used again:

Analyze the picture. Generate BPMN xml code that I can import into a bpmn editor. Don't forget the bpmndi\:BPMNDiagram section to ensure that the graphical information is included.

Imagine what it would have taken just a short time ago to convert that image into an editable BPMN model. You would need complex algorithms for object recognition, some graph theory, and capable programmers to implement the whole thing. Now everyone can do this with just one prompt. Here's the result:

Process "Contract Request" in SAP Cloud ALM, generated by Claude 3.5

Once again, the result has not been edited. If you don't believe it - just try it out! The free versions of ChatGPT-4o and Claude 3.5 sonnet are sufficient to generate one or two models per day.

Process Modeling with SAP Signavio

SAP Cloud ALM covers all areas of application lifecycle management and includes a simple BPMN editor for modeling business processes. If you tend to use the process models provided by SAP and model only a few business processes yourself, this is often sufficient.

However, if you want to model company-wide business processes in teams or use individual workflows and approval processes, SAP Signavio Process Manager is the way to go. Unlike SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio supports full BPMN 2 and even has functions for process simulations. A comprehensive list of all the benefits is beyond the scope of this blog post, so it's best to take a look at the SAP Signavio Process Manager homepage. You need a license to use the SAP Signavio Process Manager, but you can also request a 30-day trial version. There is a free version for students as part of the "Academics Initiative".

To import BPMN xml models, switch first to the Process Manager. Here you can import BPMN xml files:

SAP Signavio Process Manager, import BPMN

It has proven to be extremely helpful that SAP Signavio displays errors or warnings during the import. This allows you to detect errors during generation and adjust your prompt if necessary.

Comparison of AI models used

I tried out several LLMs for this blog:

  • ChatGPT-3.5
  • ChatGPT-4 and 4o
  • Claude 3.5 sonnet

I also tested a few open source LLMs that I installed locally on my computer:

  • Llama3:8b
  • Llava:7b
  • gemma2:9b

With the available open source LLMs running at acceptable speeds on my machine, I have had no luck. ChatGPT-3.5 could sometimes generate valid xml code, but the visual layout was never satisfactory. The best results were delivered by ChatGPT-4 and the VLMs ChatGPT-4o (released May 2024) and Claude 3.5 Sonnet (released in June 2024). ChatGPT-4o was able to generate all models correctly, but Claude 3.5 was slightly twice as fast, and the graphical layout was usually more accurate.

Conclusion

My experiments showed that you can generate business process models from a simple textual description, from tables, from pictures, or even from hand-drawn whiteboard graphics. At the time of writing this blog, ChatGPT-4o and Claude 3.5 Sonnet have only been available for a few weeks. These impressive results are only possible with these LLMs.

What is your experience? Have you been able to successfully generate BPMN models, or have you discovered other LLMs that work? Thanks for reading and let me know what you think in the comments below.

entry.title: ABAP list's column width are not uniform.
entry.content:
Hi, I have a custom program that was ran on background. It created an ABAP list. However the width of the columns were not the same size. I have tried to use the output type and its format on different program but it's ABAP list's column width were uniformed or of the same length.

 

As you can see below, the line #3 or the column of Kareem Abdul-Jabbar is longer that the first two. How do I keep them in uniform length?

entry.title: Re: Enabling Custom/Customer Namespace Table Maintenance via MDC for Business Partner
entry.content: Good Information. Thank you

entry.title: Re: Be Comfortable, Authentic and Interview the Interviewer 🧑🏻‍💻
entry.content: Very good insight and really full with motivation.

entry.title: Re: How can I restrict access to the Bank details on Business Partner?
entry.content:
Dear Amir

Thank you for your feedback in 2020, however I am still facing a similar issue.

As you explained, it is possible to restrict a users access to either display or maintain bank details via the business partner (we use the BP transaction instead of the FIORI apps). My customer however is looking for a way to a more in-depth solution by restricting the user to either display or maintain, while in maintenance view the user is only allowed to enter bank details of existing banks.

For example, if the user is entering a bank account of a bank that is existing via FI02/FI03, that the input is possible. If the bank is not yet maintained in FI01/FI02, the input should not be possible.

Please advise, if there is a current solution for this issue.

Cheers

Rico

entry.title: Re: Action button and workflow in QM notification
entry.content: When it will be activated and in which business process pls

entry.title: Re: SOC Standard Occupational Classification Tracking
entry.content: I added a customer influence request for this if you would like to review and vote for it. https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/326762

entry.title: Re: Standard Occupational Classification in SuccessFactors
entry.content: I have added a customer influence request for this field as well https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/326762

entry.title: Re: Valuated stock in transit in Advanced Intercompany Sales
entry.content:
Hi Adel,

for Adv ICo Sales using valuated stock in transit in the delivering company and selling company is not optional.

Running Advanced ICo Sales without valuated stock in transit is not possible and there is currently also no plan to support it.

The respective goods movements can be automated, but of course you need to create necessary material master data.

Without valuated stock in transit in the selling company advanced ICo sales would have severe issues with seamless integration into financial accouting, e.g. product costing in the selling company, group valuation, event-based revenue recognition, consolidation, etc. 

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Work Zone: Integrate SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud content to SAP Mobile Start
entry.content:
Good day Amith,

Very good article and explained well - I followed the steps and it has mostly worked.

I have the same issue as Anupam where I am prompted for credentials:

Have you been able to resolve this issue?

If yes, how did you resolve?

Regards,

Brandon

entry.title: Re: How to add PR items in a existing RFQ
entry.content: Hi Sridhar, In this scenario, please create a new RFQ for additional PR line items. Currently, it is not possible to manually add new PR line items to an existing RFQ.

entry.title: VX11N IN S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi,

is it possible to use VX11N  in S/4 Hana without using TRM.

can we copy the program-SAPMV86E(VX11N) to Z and make a Z transaction for capturing the Letter of Credit values without using TRM in S/4 Hana.

 

Thank you

Siva

entry.title: Re: OData Service Binding: Deleted but still shown
entry.content:
Might be useful for those who'll find this thread by searching for the same issue.

I've made the following way to delete a binding that doesn't exist:

  1. Create a service definition with anything inside
  2. Create a service binding with the same name as the binding we are trying to delete and the created service definition. It will be published after activation
  3. Unpublish the created service binding
  4. Goto tcode /IWBEP/REG_SERVICE
  5. Find the binding you've just created and push the button "Delete service"
  6. After this delete the binding from the package 
  7. Delete the service definition

entry.title: Message No. QV164 - Inspection Lot created via Action BOX not updated in data base
entry.content:
I have created one inspection lot from the Quality notification via the Action BOX - QMLR_CREATE_INSP_LOT.

Its displaying in notification but not in QA02 or QA03

Throwing error like this any idea?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: VX11N IN S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Please don't post to Questions about SAP Websites (issue with community Website, etc.) if it's a question about SAP software. Instead, ask in Technology Q&A: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/qa-p/technology-questions.

Hopefully someone will fix it for you.

entry.title: Re: Other CDS View for A_EquipmentPartner
entry.content: Added S/4HANA Cloud for Extensibility tag, Please remove other tags.

entry.title: Configuring Microsoft SSO and Conditional Authentication in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
In this blog, we will discover how to integrate SAP Cloud Identity Services with Microsoft Entra ID. By integrating these two platforms, you can:

  • Manage access to SAP Cloud Identity Services directly from Microsoft Entra ID.
  • Enable seamless, automatic sign-in to SAP Cloud Identity Services using Microsoft Entra accounts.
  • Consolidate account management into a single centralized location.

Requirements

  1. Configure Microsoft Entra SSO in SAP SF IAS.
  2. Conditional Authentication for internal employees using both Microsoft accounts and external credentials.

Step 1 - Download the Metadata file from IAS

The metadata file from Identity Authentication Service(IAS) can be downloaded as below:

  • Sign in to the SAP Cloud Identity Services administration console. The URL has the following pattern - https://<tenant-id>.accounts.ondemand.com/admin

  • In the Single Sign-On tab, go to SAML 2.0 Configuration, click the Download Metadata File button to download the metadata, and use it later in the Entra side configuration.

  • "Download Metadata File" will be available. Click on the button and the file will be generated.

    This metadata file needs to be shared with Microsoft Entra admin.

Step 2 - Configure Microsoft Entra SSO

  • Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center as at least a Cloud Application Administrator.
  • Browse to Identity > Applications > Enterprise applications > SAP Cloud Identity Services > Single sign-on.
  • On the Select a single sign-on method page, select SAML.
  • On the Set up single sign-on with SAML page, click the pencil icon for Basic SAML Configuration to edit the settings.

  •  Click on the 'Edit' button. You will get an option to upload the metadata file. Upload the metadata file received in part 1 from IAS.

  • After the metadata file is successfully uploaded, the Identifier and Reply URL values get auto-populated in the Basic SAML Configuration section.
  • You might need to update Sign-on URL manually. Sign-on URL will be(please note "tenant id" name can be checked from tenant settings): https://<tenant-id>.accounts.ondemand.com/saml2/idp/sso/<tenant-id>.accounts.ondemand.com 
  • Click on Save.
  • On the Set up Single Sign-On with SAML page, in the SAML Signing Certificate section, click Download to download the Metadata XML.

Step 3 - Assign the Microsoft Entra test user

Test users needs to be assigned to authenticate via SSO.

  • Sign in to the Microsoft Entra admin center as at least a Cloud Application Administrator

  • Browse to Identity > Applications > Enterprise applications > SAP Cloud Identity Services.
  • In the app's overview page, find the Manage section and select Users and groups.
  • Select Add user, then select Users and Groups in the Add Assignment dialog.
  • In the Users and groups dialog, select all required employees from the Users list, then click the Select button at the bottom of the screen.
  • If you expect a role to be assigned to the users, you can select it from the Select a role dropdown. If no role has been set up for this app, you see the "Default Access" role selected.
  • In the Add Assignment dialog, click the Assign button.

Step 4 - Configure SAP Cloud Identity Services SSO

  • Under Identity Providers, choose the Corporate Identity Providers tile.

  •  Create a new identity provider, and give a display name. Update Identity Provider Type = Microsoft ADFS/Entra ID (SAML 2.0) and Click on the Create button.

  •  Once created, Click on Application, Go to Trust -> SAML 2.0 Configuration, and click on Browse to upload the Metadata XML file which we have downloaded from the Entra configuration (step 2). Click Save.

  • Update Identity Federation settings for User Store.

Step 5 - Conditional Authenctication Rules

As per the business requirements, only employees should authenticate via SSO, and contractors should authenticate via password. This can be achieved as follows:

  • Navigate to Applications & Resources -> Applications -> Click on SAP SuccessFactors system -> Conditional Authentication

  •  Create a condition to authenticate via a specific identity provider. Please note the default is still IAS.

  • This condition can be set up by either email domain/User Type/User Group. for demo purposes, an SSO group is created where accounts have been added to authenticate via SSO. All others who are not part of this group will authenticate via the default provider which is IAS.

This can be automated to assign users to authenticate via SSO using custom solutions and setups.

Conclusion

By following the steps outlined in this blog, we can successfully integrate SAP Cloud Identity Services with Microsoft Entra ID, providing a seamless and secure Single Sign-On (SSO) experience for different users. This integration allows us to manage access to SAP Cloud Identity Services directly from Microsoft Entra ID, enabling automatic sign-ins and centralized account management.

Additionally, implementing Conditional Authentication ensures that internal employees can use their Microsoft accounts for SSO, while external accounts authenticate with credentials.

In summary, the integration of Microsoft Entra ID with SAP Cloud Identity Services, coupled with Conditional Authentication, optimizes the SAP SuccessFactors environment, making it more secure and user-friendly.

Hope this helps 🙂 Happy Learning!

Please feel free to drop a comment if you have any other ideas or solutions that helped customise the business needs 🙂

Please do not hestiate to reach out if you have any questions or queries!

entry.title: Re: VX11N IN S/4 HANA

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano,

I identify as a person in the below categories:

    • People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts 
    • First generation college students

I hold a Master's Degree in Business and a Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering. I want to work as a consultant and have good knowledge in the SCM (Supply Chain Management) domain. Along with functional knowledge, I want to gain some technical expertise which will help me grow as a TechnoFunctional person. SAP Certification will be a great benefit for me.

Also requesting experienced fellows to guide me choose certification path.

Thanks & Regards,
Rutu

I believe that we need template to be able to use IFRS and add additional tags if needed.  We need to produce inline XBRL fin statements so do we need mapping method as well?  From the notes it seems so but it's not clear.

Thanks in advance

Eyal

entry.title: Re: ABAP list's column width are not uniform.

entry.title: Cycle Count IM
entry.content:
Hi,

We are implementing Cycle count process using IM (don't have EWM). We assigned CC Inv Code (A.B C) to all materials. Now when we schedule print Count documents (for the first time) it prints all active materials. It is not possible to cycle count all active parts, our question is what is the best practice for implementing Cycle Count in the middle of the year? Our understating is that because we never cycle counted before therefore system is
selecting all materials for counting, but its not possible to count all. 
Thanks in advance for any comments / suggestion.
Ashraf

S/4HANA Public Cloud 2402

entry.title: Re: can I connect SAP Datasphere to Priority Software?
entry.content:
Hello Deb_D, 

I guess the answer depends on what connection options are offered on the Priority Software end. 

You can see the connection options from the SAP Datasphere end via Preparing Connectivity for Connections | SAP Help Portal. The OData option might work if they have an OData api to connect to. Also Generic JDBC may be an option. 

Regards
Mark

entry.title: Does Cloud ALM support ChaRM
entry.content:
• Does ALM have ChaRM?
  • How does ALM operate ChaRM if there is such?

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Does Cloud ALM support ChaRM
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

To understand Change Request Management in Cloud ALM, kindly refer to the presentations and recordings of the last ALM Summit EMEA 2023 : https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-almsummit/sap-alm-summit-emea-2023-materials.html?sap-outbound-id=BB15CA9BEFBA63B6493D9B98CAE8008C3B83C5DE&smc_campaign_id=0000037414&source=email-smc

In particular, Check presentation : What's in for You? Get the Latest Outlook for SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation

Regarding the question on : To connect to On-prem we see you only need you ST-PI tools to be up to date, no installation is required, who configures ALM to talk to the On-prem system?" : refer to SAP Community blog First steps to work with SAP Cloud ALM Deployment scenario for SAP ABAP systems (7.40 or higher) 

There are additional configuration required to work with the Managed systems in Cloud ALM. 

Best Regards,
Deepa

entry.title: Behavior validation messages are not displayed
entry.content:
Validation error messages in a behavior implementation are not displayed and elements are not highlighted. 

In the behavior implementation for a draft enabled RAP based Fiori Elements application there is a custom check for mandatory fields. If a mandatory field is not filled, an error message should be displayed and the relevant element should be highlighted. The validation is executed correctly and the save action is stopped but only a generic error message ("Resolve data inconsistencies to save changes.") is displayed and no field is highlighted. When the app runs in preview mode via the Service Binding in Eclipse, the validation messages are correctly displayed and the relevant fields highlighted. The issue only occurs if the app runs in the Fiori Launchpad.

Behavior definition: 

managed implementation in class zbp_i_product unique;
strict ( 2 );
with draft;

define behavior for ZI_Product alias product
persistent table za_product
draft table zd_product
authorization master ( instance )
lock master
total etag LastChangedAt
etag master LastChangedAt
{
  create;
  update;
  delete;

  field ( mandatory ) ProductName;
  field ( readonly, numbering : managed ) ProductUuid;
  field ( readonly ) CreatedBy, CreatedAt, LastChangedBy, LastChangedAt;

  validation validateMandatoryFields on save { field ProductName create; }

  mapping for za_product
    {
      ProductUuid = product_uuid;
      ProductName = product_name;
      ProductType = product_type;
      CreatedBy = created_by;
      CreatedAt = created_at;
      LastChangedBy = last_changed_by;
      LastChangedAt = last_changed_at;
    }

  draft determine action Prepare
  {
    validation validateMandatoryFields;
  }
  draft action Edit;
  draft action Activate optimized;
  draft action Discard;
  draft action Resume;

}

Behavior implementation: 

CLASS lhc_product DEFINITION INHERITING FROM cl_abap_behavior_handler.
  PRIVATE SECTION.
    METHODS validatemandatoryfields FOR VALIDATE ON SAVE
      IMPORTING keys FOR product~validatemandatoryfields.
ENDCLASS.


CLASS lhc_product IMPLEMENTATION.
 METHOD validatemandatoryfields.
    READ ENTITIES OF /zi_product IN LOCAL MODE
         ENTITY product
         FIELDS ( ProductName )
         WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )
         RESULT DATA(products).

    LOOP AT products ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<product>).
      APPEND VALUE #( %tky        = <product>-%tky
                      %state_area = 'VALIDATE_MANDATORY' )
             TO reported-product.

      IF <product>-ProductName IS INITIAL.
        APPEND VALUE #( %tky = <product>-%tky ) TO failed-product.
        APPEND VALUE #( %tky              = <product>-%tky
                        %state_area       = 'VALIDATE_MANDATORY'
                        %msg              = new_message( id = `ZPRODUCT`
                                                         number = 009 
                                                         severity = if_abap_behv_message=>severity-error )
                        %element-ProductName= if_abap_behv=>mk-on ) TO reported-product.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.
  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

The Fiori app is created as a Fiori Elements List Report based on an OData V4 service.

I checked with a colleagues and it is possible to delete additional ids. See our online documentation Function Imports | SAP Help Portal. Another method is to set opt-out permissions for the contacts. See documentation Structure of OData Service API_MKT_CONTACTS | SAP Help Portal > Marketing Permissions.

Can't one of those methods be used?

Best Regards, Isabelle

entry.title: BP Master Migration Template
entry.content:
For a SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud setting, in transaction "BP", it is possible to set an expiration date to a BP's ID information via field "Valid to". At the same time, this field is not found within "Customer" migration XML template. How to migrate this field via Migration Cockpit in this scenario?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BP Master Migration Template
entry.content:
When talking specifically about a SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud context, object customization is a little bit more restrictive. It is also important to keep in mind that whichever fields are available, they were individually evaluated and chosen by the respective businesses areas to form the currently available migration object.

With that said, there are certain flexibility offered via SAP note 2999428. This notes points to (public cloud) migration objects for which certain customization is possible. From the objects, it is possible to get details on what specific fields are available to be included.

2999428 - Collective Note for extensions via Model Your Migration Objects - Migration Object Modeler App in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition (Migrate Data Using Staging Tables)

In case either the desired migration object AND/OR the desirable field(s) are not on the list, the path forward would be to file for a missing feature, following the documentation below:

2676589 - Request for new features in SAP S/4HANA Data Migration Content
11 - Would you like to shape & influence future releases of SAP Products?

You can find more information about the SAP Customer Influence program at the following page:

http://influence.sap.com

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Plain "REST" endpoint (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content: Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Task 4 - Plain "REST" endpoint (July Developer Challenge - "Reverse APIs")
entry.content: Nice

entry.title: Re: WebI Login taking 30 seconds+
entry.content: Issue was resolved when the instances tile added a filter for instance time of "today/yesterday" only as part of an SAP patch.  We had hundreds of thousands of saved instances to the server as this is the default option.  So the system was attempting to query/parse all those instances to front load the data before the tile was selected.  After a serious round of cleanup of data and the institution of only showing "yesterday/today" in the instance time; the logon delay was alleviated.

entry.title: 35 Healthy Smoothie Recipes That'll Keep You Fueled All Day
entry.content:
What is the best way to build a healthy smoothie?

First, think nutrient- and vitamin-rich produce. Smoothies are a great way to make use of leftover fresh fruit or to sneak in some extra superfoods that you wouldn't normally eat on their own (like frozen papaya or dragonfruit). Think beyond the classic add-ins and consider dark greens like kale, a squeeze of citrus or spices like  and turmeric (try them both freshly grated!). Herbs like fresh basil and mint make a tasty addition, and  if your blender is strong enough  hearty veggies like carrots and beets. Then add some protein, like plain  or cottage cheese, for an energy boost that will keep you going until lunch. For a more filling smoothie, consider including healthy fats like nut butter or avocado.

entry.title: Re: Issue while creating custom client in SAP MDK
12 Healthy Eating Memes We Can All Relate To
entry.content:
Ear clean is a lifestyle, and we do it because we feel our best when we load up on the veggies, fruits, whole grains, lean proteins, nuts, and seeds. When we eat tons of sugar, refined carbs, alcohol, and packaged foods made with 38 ingredients we can't pronounce, we have low energy, brain fog, and don't sleep well.

That's not to say that eating clean is 100 percent easy and awesome every second of the day. If you strive to eat clean most of the time, but every once in a while indulge in your beloved pizza , then these memes will speak volumes of truth to you

entry.title: serial number status is not updating in IQ09

entry.title: Re: serial number status is not updating in IQ09
entry.content: The KBA https://launchpad.support.sap.com/#/notes/3440283 'System Status appears as blank in the app 'Display Material Serial Number ' (IQ09)' explains the logic and how to get the System Status to appear

entry.title: Re: Is anyone having issues with learning items not opening?
entry.content: Hi Mike, we are currently experiencing something similar after making a set of elearning modules with Adobe Captivate. I'd be happy to chat in more detail and share some screenshots of the issue - essentially the user click 'Launch Content' and the pop up box opens but the content never loads. But it doesn't affect everyone. My assumption was it the file size originally but we can't find any theme, pattern or commonality between those users where is does work and those where it doesn't! Let me know if you want some more information, Thanks, Emily

entry.title: Re: Oracle tables not available in BW/4HANA(under HANA_SD...

The team have checked and the user on the Oracle database seems to have all the required permission. They are still checking n this further, Till now no resolution.

entry.title: Re: How to add "Audience" to the oauth token method used in charon integration
entry.content: Charon has its history tied to the YaaS (Hybris as a Service) model from around 2014/2015. It was one of the first declarative rest clients out there. If you are looking for a declarative rest client that is more full featured, I’d recommend looking at Feign client. As a caveat, I am not sure if Feign client is compatible with SAP Commerce’s Spring architecture.

entry.title: Re: Oracle tables not available in BW/4HANA(under HANA_SD...
entry.content: Please look into this blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/using-sap-hana-smart-data-access-to-get-hana-and-oracle-working-together/ba-p/13547003

entry.title: How to read .plv file for ABAP CDS view performance analysis and tie it back to CDS view?
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have been going over various blogs on 'how to analyse performance of the ABAP CDS view'. Although there is enough documentation on the steps to perform the analysis, but there isn't any documentation on how to read/analyse the .plv file in HANA Planviz perspective and tie it back to the CDS view.

As in, which join/which line/which underlying cds view is causing performance issue?

What is the threshold (in ms) for a step to be considered for a performance analysis?

Most of the times when an analytical query, built on a CDS view is run for performance test, it isn't captured properly in the ST05 trace. Then a work around is to capture the technical name of the analytical query prefix it with 2C and run in tcode-rsrt and capture the log in ST05 trace. All these issues impact the analysis process. 

Any solution to the above issues or directing to a better/simpler process for CDS view performance analyses is greatly appreciated.

Awaiting for some constructive responses. Thank You.

entry.title: Re: #EntityProviderException: Tag with name 'Edmx' was not found.
entry.content: Check https://me.sap.com/notes/0003456129 This solved my issue.

entry.title: Re: Story Reports - Classic Design Experience (CDE) - Deprecation Warning
entry.content:
Hello Conny,

To your question about Stories functionality, yes there will be a retirement of the Classic Experience reports in favor of using the Optimized Story Experience.   This is expected sometime in 2025, but we yet to have full details from the SAC team.  More information can be found on their blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/important-notice-changes-in-sap-analytics-cloud-classic-stories-amp/ba-p/13705220

We also have a blog showing how simple it is to convert to OSE, please check out the content there too:

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/stories-in-people-analytics-optimized-story-experience-ose/ba-p/227281 

Thanks!
Ben

entry.title: Re: Story Reports - Classic Design Experience (CDE) - Dep...
entry.content:
Hello Conny,

To your question about Stories functionality, yes there will be a retirement of the Classic Experience reports in favor of using the Optimized Story Experience.   This is expected sometime in 2025, but we yet to have full details from the SAC team.  More information can be found on their blog:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/important-notice-changes-in-sap-analytics-cloud-classic-stories-amp/ba-p/13705220

We also have a blog showing how simple it is to convert to OSE, please check out the content there too:

https://community.sap.com/t5/product-and-customer-updates/stories-in-people-analytics-optimized-story-experience-ose/ba-p/227281 

Thanks!
Ben

entry.title: Can not login to SAP Service Layer. Please Help
entry.content:
Hello

When I POST to https://SAP-BO-SERVER:50000/b1s/v1/Login

{
    "CompanyDB":"VCC",
    "UserName":"manager",
    "Password":"1111"
}
SAP Service Layer return error
{
    "error": {
        "code": 100000027,
        "message": {
            "lang": "en-us",
            "value": "Error while connecting to database server SAP-BO-SERVER\n"
        }
    }
}
Please help me fix it.
Thank you very much. Sorry for my English

entry.title: Is RRI available in AO for S/4?
entry.content: I have a requirement to have a report-to-report Interface in an Analysis for Office (2.8.16) report that renders data from KSB1. This once worked on ECC 7.0 but does not seem to work for S/4. Is this still possible? If so, can someone be so kind as to provide step by step instructions for an old BW Admin now working with AO and Bex?

entry.title: Requested data was not found - Fiori object page
entry.content:
Hello, I have a list report application, and when I click onto the object page, I sometimes get the error "Requested data was not found":

On the object page, all there is, is a Sales doc, and sold to party information in the header, and all sales documents have that info, so it's not that the data itself doesn't exist.
I have found the different circumstances under which this error page appears:

1. If the data for the header is not on the list report column, then it gives the error, e.g.:

Since the table does not have sold to party, then I get the error on the object page, if I do include the column, then I don't get the error on the next page:

 -> 

The second, and more important set of circumstance when I get the error, is if a column does not have any info, e.g.:

Since subsequent document is empty for this contract (i.e. the contract was not renewed), the error appears. But for my table I will have columns that will sometimes have no info (like Subsequent Document), but I will also need to be able to click on to the object page. Does anyone have a solution for getting this "Requested data not found" error on the object page?

entry.title: Re: Scheduling after Activity Confirmation in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content: Hi, if activities/WBS are scheduled in the past or not highly depends on the settings (see the indicator "start in the past" in Kens post).

entry.title: Re: Using OData in a Ui-Integration Card (Component)
entry.content: Hello!

entry.title: Re: Scheduling after Activity Confirmation in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Exactly, that's what I also always recommend to do in such scenarios.

In addition you have to reschedule the WBS/project in order to push the dates bottom-up to the WBS. Else only the activity will keep the actual dates. If you don't want to do that manually, the indicator "automatic scheduling at saving" may fulfill that. But that depends on your overall project scheduling processes, so it should be handled with care.

entry.title: Application Consultant SAP PP - Hybrid (Heidenheim, Germany)
entry.content:
HARTMANN GROUP, seeking a highly skilled Application Consultant SAP PP to join our dynamic IT team. In this role, you will be responsible for analyzing requirements, designing, configuring, and operating IT solutions based on standard processes. Your expertise will ensure the availability and scalability of our applications, contributing to the ongoing modernization and optimization of our IT landscape.

 Responsibilities:

  • Analyze requirements, design, configure, and operate IT solutions according to IT standard processes.
  • Understand, analyze, and document requirements; recommend appropriate solutions to ensure application availability and scalability.
  • Operate relevant IT applications and solutions in line with agreed service levels.
  • Select suitable hardware, software, and service providers to meet business needs.
  • Define architecture and platform or software basis for implementing and provisioning IT solutions.
  • Drive standardization, harmonization, and modernization of applications.
  • Identify process optimization and innovation potentials using IT tools and digitalization capabilities.
  • Take ownership of service delivery for respective solutions and services.

Qualifications:

  • University degree in IT or equivalent field.
  • Several years of practical experience with global applications in the assigned area.
  • Training/certification in one or more IT delivery processes (e.g., SCRUM, PMP, Prince, ITIL) is a plus.
  • Advanced skills in IT Requirements Engineering and Design Thinking, with a basic understanding of IT architectures.
  • Strong stakeholder management skills with a proven ability to build relationships with key contacts.
  • Experience in managing service providers and partners to deliver applications aligned with business objectives.
  • Proficiency in English and German.

This is a full time (hybrid) opportunity, kindly apply to the link or reach out to me directly. you can also connect with me on LinkedIn.

https://careers.hartmann.info/job-invite/6047/?utm_source=SAPCommunity

entry.title: Re: [C4C] Disable the possibility to set Opportunity in status "lost" without a product ma
entry.content: https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-cloud-for-customer/solution-guide-for-sap-sales-cloud/scope-and-configure-opportunities#configure-system-message-severity

entry.title: Re: Can not login to SAP Service Layer. Please Help
entry.content:
On c:\Program Files\SAP\SAP Business One ServerTools\ServiceLayer\logs\

Securityevent.20240715.b1s.log

2024-07-15 23:57:46.721000 SAP-BO-SERVER:VCC:manager 10.10.20.183 LoginFailed:pid=7944:session=96A296D224F285C67BEE93C30F8A309157F0DAA35DC5B87E410B78630A09CFC7
2024-07-15 23:57:49.522000 SAP-BO-SERVER:VCC:manager 10.10.20.183 LoginFailed:pid=7944:session=96A296D224F285C67BEE93C30F8A309157F0DAA35DC5B87E410B78630A09CFC7

 

 

error_50000_log_2024_07_15

[Mon Jul 15 23:40:46.271145 2024] [ssl:warn] [pid 7992:tid 488] AH01906: www.sap.com:50000:0 server certificate is a CA certificate (BasicConstraints: CA == TRUE !?)
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:46.271145 2024] [ssl:warn] [pid 7992:tid 488] AH01909: www.sap.com:50000:0 server certificate does NOT include an ID which matches the server name
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:46.739871 2024] [ssl:warn] [pid 7992:tid 488] AH01906: www.sap.com:50000:0 server certificate is a CA certificate (BasicConstraints: CA == TRUE !?)
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:46.739871 2024] [ssl:warn] [pid 7992:tid 488] AH01909: www.sap.com:50000:0 server certificate does NOT include an ID which matches the server name
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:46.739871 2024] [mpm_winnt:notice] [pid 7992:tid 488] AH00455: Apache/2.4.43 (Win64) OpenSSL/1.0.2u configured -- resuming normal operations
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:46.739871 2024] [mpm_winnt:notice] [pid 7992:tid 488] AH00456: Server built: Aug 6 2020 12:14:54
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:46.739871 2024] [core:notice] [pid 7992:tid 488] AH00094: Command line: 'C:\\Program Files\\SAP\\SAP Business One ServerTools\\ServiceLayer\\Apache\\bin\\httpd.exe -d C:/Program Files/SAP/SAP Business One ServerTools/ServiceLayer/Apache -f ..\\conf\\httpd-b1s-lb.conf'
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:46.755510 2024] [mpm_winnt:notice] [pid 7992:tid 488] AH00418: Parent: Created child process 8552
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:48.036712 2024] [ssl:warn] [pid 8552:tid 200] AH01906: www.sap.com:50000:0 server certificate is a CA certificate (BasicConstraints: CA == TRUE !?)
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:48.052338 2024] [ssl:warn] [pid 8552:tid 200] AH01909: www.sap.com:50000:0 server certificate does NOT include an ID which matches the server name
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:48.396140 2024] [ssl:warn] [pid 8552:tid 200] AH01906: www.sap.com:50000:0 server certificate is a CA certificate (BasicConstraints: CA == TRUE !?)
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:48.396140 2024] [ssl:warn] [pid 8552:tid 200] AH01909: www.sap.com:50000:0 server certificate does NOT include an ID which matches the server name
[Mon Jul 15 23:40:48.396140 2024] [mpm_winnt:notice] [pid 8552:tid 200] AH00354: Child: Starting 64 worker threads.
[Mon Jul 15 23:41:50.123883 2024] [proxy_balancer:error] [pid 8552:tid 1108] [client 127.0.0.1:49847] AH10187: ignoring params in balancer-manager cross-site access
[Mon Jul 15 23:42:01.819168 2024] [proxy_balancer:error] [pid 8552:tid 1108] [client 127.0.0.1:49852] AH10187: ignoring params in balancer-manager cross-site access

entry.title: Re: PEO: Parameter Effectivity for Major Assembly Project...
entry.content:
Hi,

please implement note 3458785.

 

regards,

Ninad Narad

entry.title: Re: Can I dynamically switch between Screen Personas based on actions taken on-screen?
entry.content:
Of course. You can switch the flavor via scripting, based on input value.
session.utils.changeFlavor

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
"Dear SAP DSI Team,

 

I am writing to express my keen interest in the SAP Digital Skills Initiative (DSI). As a young and ambitious Egyptian professional i fall under this category

 

People in remote or underserved locations that may be not have access to the same economic opportunities as their urban or more privileged counterparts. 

I see this program as a perfect springboard to transition from HR analysis to HCM consulting and reporting, specifically with SAP SuccessFactors People Analytics & Reporting.

 

My passion lies in utilizing technology to foster a more inclusive and efficient work environment within the HR domain. This program presents a pivotal opportunity for me to embark on a fulfilling career shift.

 

My extensive experience in HR, combined with the technical expertise gained through the DSI, will equip me to effectively implement technological solutions. This program will not only solidify my technical knowledge but also empower me to drive impactful changes within organizations by leveraging the strategic potential of SAP SuccessFactors and well-informed decision-making processes.

 

Beyond personal growth, I am particularly enthusiastic about the DSI's potential to position me as a role model and advocate for diversity within the tech industry. I am eager to contribute my unique perspective while actively acquiring new knowledge.

 

Thank you for your time and consideration. I am confident that my skills and aspirations align perfectly with the DSI program, and I look forward to the opportunity to discuss my qualifications 

Best regards  

@David

entry.title: Re: How to avoid empty drafts in RAP?
entry.content:
you can use augment

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/implement-consumption-specific-behavior-in-abap-rap/ba-p/13576934

 

or use action to create new instance

entry.title: Re: ODBC 18 Driver - Invalid Argument for Database
entry.content: Just wanted to report back that using the new CR SP36, those issues with ODBC 18 have been fixed 🙂 We will keep testing and THANK YOU!

entry.title: Re: "OTF end command // missing in OTF data" while converting smartform to pdf
entry.content: Could you indicate how you resolved it? I also have the same problem

entry.title: Re: SUBMIT rkaep000 for KOB1 - in Background
entry.content: Hi Nagaseshu. Did you received the code for this KOB1 to display alv?

entry.title: Re: Problem to change or assign payee of supplier documen...
entry.content:
Hey Guti,

This particular functionality is similar in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and On Premise.

I was getting the same result you shared in your screenshots (thank you for adding them since it makes a lot easier to understand the question!) and I was able to “see/enable” the “Payee” field.

The visibility of the "Payer" field in this particular app is related to the setup of the Supplier Master data. Specifically, you need to have Alternative Payees configured for the supplier in question. This enables the app to display additional fields that are pertinent to the payment transaction details.

Here's how you can solve this:

1. Navigate to the Supplier Master data screen.

2. Enter the relevant details for Alternative Payees within the supplier’s profile. Once you have added or confirmed these details, revisit the "Change Document" App. You should now see that the "Payer" field becomes visible and editable, allowing you to proceed with your tasks.

I've included a few screenshots from a test I performed in a SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. These images should guide you through the process step-by-step. Please take your time to review them and replicate the steps in your system.

I hope this helps.

Ernesto Cepeda

entry.title: Re: Unable to see the negative values for the Reversed Ma...
entry.content: Hi Marius,

entry.title: Why negative credit exposure in open orders and deliveries.
entry.content:
Hi all, 

I have been having an issue in which negative values ​​are appearing in customers' credit exposure. When this happens I run the UKM_COMMITMENT_REBUILD program, but after 1 month it happens again.

Does anyone know why this happens and if there is any way to prevent this from happening?
